document: 1. FSCA Conduct Standard 1 of 2025 - Requirements for FIs providing FE initiatives

sections.heading: Definitions
sections.summary: This section defines key terms used throughout the Conduct Standard, including 'the Act', 'Authority', 'evaluation', 'financial customer', 'financial education', 'financial education initiative', 'Financial Sector Code', 'GN500', 'monitoring', 'service provider', and 'target group'. These definitions establish the scope and meaning of critical concepts for financial institutions providing financial education initiatives, ensuring clarity in subsequent obligations.

requirements.text: In this Schedule, 'the Act' means the Financial Sector Regulation Act, 2017
'Authority' means the Financial Sector Conduct Authority as defined in section 1 of the Act
'evaluation' means a systematic and objective assessment of an ongoing or completed financial education initiative, its design, implementation and results
'financial customer' has the meaning assigned to it in the Act, but also includes a potential financial customer

'financial education' means the process by which financial customers improve their understanding of financial products, financial product providers, financial services, financial service providers, financial concepts and risks and, through objective basic information, instruction and the like, aim to develop the skills and confidence to become more aware of financial risks and opportunities; make informed financial decision; manage their financial affairs more sustainably; know where to go for financial assistance and recourse; or take other effective actions to improve their financial well-being and the financial well-being of those under their responsibility

'financial education initiative' means any financial education programme, or other similar initiative or activity that is aimed at providing financial education, excluding any such initiative that is aimed at marketing or creating awareness surrounding a specific financial product or service, financial product provider or financial service provider
'Financial Sector Code' means the codes of good practice on broad based black economic empowerment issued by the Department of Trade and Industry under Government Notice

'GN500' means Guidance Notice GN500(A) on Code Series FS500, Statement 500 - the measurement of the consumer education element of broad-based black economic empowerment, issued by the Financial Sector Transformation Council and referenced in the FSC
'monitoring' means the regular collection and analysis of information about the progress of a financial education initiative

'service provider' means any person, whether or not that person is a representative or other agent of a financial institution, with whom a financial institution has an arrangement relating to the provision of financial education initiatives on the financial institution's behalf
'specific financial product or service' means the actual financial product provided by a financial product provider or actual financial service provided by a financial services provider

'target group' means any identifiable person or group of people, for example: youth; women; small, medium and micro-enterprises; older persons/pensioners; people living with disabilities; previously disadvantaged; underserved groups; people of a specific race; over-indebted individuals; individuals unable to repay debts; individuals unable to maintain insurance policy payments; and/or any other group characterised by an identified vulnerability

sections.source_quote: In this Schedule, 'the Act' means the Financial Sector Regulation Act, 2017 () and any word or expression to which a meaning has been assigned in the Act bears the meaning so assigned to it, and unless the context otherwise indicates –

sections.heading: Application, purpose and proportional application
sections.summary: This section specifies that the Conduct Standard applies only when financial institutions provide or offer financial education initiatives, including those delivered by service providers. It outlines the purpose of ensuring financial customers' protection through appropriate financial education initiatives and requires financial institutions to take reasonable steps for governance and oversight in developing, implementing, monitoring, and evaluating initiatives while reporting effectiveness to the Authority. The requirements are proportional to the institution's size, complexity, and risk profile.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes that financial education initiatives must be designed and implemented with appropriate governance and oversight to ensure effectiveness and compliance with regulatory reporting, which impacts data transfer and processing across borders for financial institutions.

requirements.text: This Conduct Standard is only applicable where a financial institution provides or offers a financial education initiative, including the provision of a financial education initiative by a service provider

The purpose of this Conduct Standard is aimed at ensuring the protection of financial customers by ensuring that financial education initiatives provided by financial institutions are appropriate and support the ability of financial customers to make more informed and/or sound financial decisions about their personal finances or others that fall under their responsibility, as well as the financial health of their small, medium and micro-enterprises

The purpose of this Conduct Standard is aimed at ensuring that a financial institution providing financial education initiatives takes reasonable steps to ensure appropriate standards of behaviour, governance and oversight when developing content, implementing, monitoring and evaluating a financial education initiative; and reporting on the effectiveness, efficiency and appropriateness of a financial education initiative, including reporting to the Authority

The requirements in this Conduct Standard, where appropriate, may be applied in a manner that is proportional to the nature, size, complexity and risk profile of the financial institution concerned as well as the nature and target group of the relevant financial education initiative

sections.source_quote: This Conduct Standard is only applicable where a financial institution provides or offers a financial education initiative, including the provision of a financial education initiative by a service provider.

sections.heading: Governance arrangements
sections.summary: This section mandates that financial institutions must establish appropriate governance arrangements to oversee the design and implementation of financial education initiatives, including ongoing monitoring and review of their suitability and effectiveness. It requires robust systems for reporting to the Authority, ensuring staff and service providers possess necessary skills, and maintaining accountability for regulatory obligations regardless of whether initiatives are delivered by the institution or a service provider.
sections.dtia_summary: Financial institutions must implement governance structures to ensure effective oversight of financial education initiatives, which impacts data handling and transfer practices across borders by requiring regular monitoring, reporting, and accountability mechanisms.

requirements.text: A financial institution must have appropriate governance arrangements in place to oversee the design and implementation of financial education initiatives

The governance arrangements referred to in subparagraph (1) must include establishing and implementing appropriate oversight arrangements to monitor and review the design, suitability and effectiveness of its financial education initiatives on an ongoing basis

A financial institution must have appropriate systems, processes, policies and controls in place to ensure appropriate reporting on financial education initiatives, pre- and post-implementation, to the Authority

A financial institution must take reasonable steps to ensure that the relevant employee and service provider responsible for the design and implementation of a financial education initiative possesses the necessary skills, knowledge and expertise to fulfil their functions

A financial institution must take reasonable steps to ensure that the governance arrangements referred to in this paragraph also apply to a service provider, including that it obtains sufficient information from that service provider, to ensure that these financial education initiatives are, and remain, effective in achieving the intended outcomes

A financial institution retains responsibility and accountability for all regulatory obligations pertaining to the provision of financial education initiatives as set out in this Conduct Standard, regardless of whether such financial education initiative is performed by the financial institution itself or by a service provider

sections.source_quote: A financial institution must have appropriate governance arrangements in place to oversee the design and implementation of financial education initiatives.

sections.heading: Design and development requirements for financial education initiatives
sections.summary: This section requires financial education initiatives to aim at enabling individuals and small businesses to make informed financial decisions and encourage positive behavioral change. It mandates that content must be based on objective information, targeted appropriately to the audience, distinct from marketing activities, and brand agnostic while unpacking financial concepts. Delivery platforms must be suitable for the target group to ensure effective reach.
sections.dtia_summary: Financial education initiatives must be designed with clear objectives and appropriate content to ensure effectiveness, which impacts data collection and processing practices across borders by requiring content to be non-marketing focused and tailored to specific vulnerable groups.

requirements.text: Financial education initiative provided by a financial institution should aim to enable natural persons and small, medium and micro-enterprises to make more informed decisions, about their own finances and financial well-being or the finances and financial well-being of others that fall under their responsibility

Financial education initiative provided by a financial institution should aim to in respect of financial decision making, encourage positive customer behaviour, influence behavioural change and contribute to long-term skills development

A financial institution may use any delivery platform for a financial education initiative, provided that the delivery platform is appropriate for the type of financial education initiative and the identified target group, to ensure appropriate and effective reach
The content of a financial education initiative provided by a financial institution must be based on objective and impartial information, generally accepted methodologies, research and trends

The content of a financial education initiative provided by a financial institution must be targeted suitably at the appropriate target group, taking into account the identified needs of the target group and financial inclusion objectives
The content of a financial education initiative provided by a financial institution must be distinct from any brand specific financial product or financial service sales process or marketing campaign

The content of a financial education initiative provided by a financial institution must be brand agnostic, but may unpack the meaning of different types of financial concepts and terms related to a particular financial product or financial service, or categories of financial products or financial services

sections.source_quote: The content of a financial education initiative provided by a financial institution must be based on objective and impartial information, generally accepted methodologies, research and trends.

sections.heading: Location and logistics
sections.summary: This section requires financial institutions to use appropriate locations for face-to-face initiatives and virtual platforms for online initiatives to ensure effectiveness. It mandates contingency plans for unforeseen circumstances and requires measurable outcomes for financial education initiatives to demonstrate effectiveness and impact. It also prohibits using initiatives for excessive marketing or sales of specific financial products.
sections.dtia_summary: Financial institutions must ensure effective delivery of financial education initiatives through appropriate locations and platforms, which impacts data storage and transfer practices across borders by requiring contingency planning and measurable outcomes.

requirements.text: A financial institution providing face-to-face financial education initiatives must utilise a location that is appropriate for this purpose to ensure that the financial education initiative is effective

A financial institution providing virtual financial education initiatives must ensure that the virtual platform is appropriate for this purpose to ensure that the financial education initiative is effective
A financial institution should consider contingency plans for unforeseen circumstances regarding implementation of financial education initiatives
The outcomes of financial education initiatives must be measurable in order to demonstrate effectiveness and measure impact
The manner used by a financial institution to measure the outcomes of each financial education initiative provided must be appropriate for the specific initiative and relate directly to the outcome

A financial institution must monitor and evaluate each financial education initiative that is provided to assess its effectiveness, identify areas for improvement, and collect data which can and should (where relevant) be used to improve the effectiveness of future programmes
A financial institution may not use a financial education initiative for excessive marketing of a specific brand or financial institution
A financial institution may not use a financial education initiative for marketing and sales of a specific financial product or financial service

sections.source_quote: A financial institution providing face-to-face financial education initiatives must utilise a location that is appropriate for this purpose to ensure that the financial education initiative is effective.
